title: The Calligrapher's Secret
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A female calligrapher in 10th-century Baghdad hides a forbidden love letter inside a holy manuscript that survives a thousand years.
long_description:
In the golden age of the Abbasid Caliphate, Layla bint Yusuf is a master calligrapher trained in secret by her father, working in the caliph's library disguised as a male apprentice named Laith. When she meets Hassan, a young Persian poet and scholar, they fall into a dangerous love that cannot exist within the strictures of court and faith.

To preserve their forbidden bond for eternity, Layla conceals a love letter — written in her most exquisite calligraphy — within an illuminated manuscript of the Mu'allaqat commissioned for the caliph himself. The letter survives centuries of conquest, empire, and neglect, hidden in plain sight.

Spanning a thousand years from 10th-century Baghdad to a modern university archive, The Calligrapher's Secret is a story about what endures: not power, not empires, but the words we leave behind. It is a meditation on love, art, and the courage to create in the face of a world that would silence you.
main_characters: Layla bint Yusuf — A master calligrapher disguised as a male apprentice named Laith
Hassan al-Farsi — A young Persian poet and scholar at the House of Wisdom
The Caliph — The Abbasid ruler who commissions the Mu'allaqat manuscript
Yusuf al-Baghdadi — Layla's father, a master calligrapher
Rashid — The chief scribe who suspects Layla's secret
Dr. Amira Nasr — A modern historian who discovers the hidden letter
